exercises.title: Kraus Operator Validator
exercises.description: Build a comprehensive validator for Kraus operator sets.
tasks: Verify trace preservation condition
Check complete positivity via Choi matrix
Validate operator dimensions match
Test on common channels (bit-flip, phase-flip, depolarizing)
Report which conditions pass or fail
exercises.expectedOutput: Kraus operator validation tool with detailed diagnostics
hints: Trace preservation: Σₖ Kₖ† Kₖ = I
Build Choi matrix and check eigenvalues ≥ 0
All Kraus operators must be square matrices of same dimension
Return structured results for each condition

exercises.title: Channel Converter
tasks: Convert Kraus operators to Choi matrix
Convert Choi matrix back to Kraus operators
Compute Pauli Transfer Matrix from Kraus
Verify round-trip conversions preserve the channel
Handle multi-qubit channels
hints: Eigendecompose Choi to get Kraus operators
Test on identity channel, depolarizing channel

exercises.title: Noise Model Library
exercises.description: Build a library of physically motivated noise models.
tasks: Implement amplitude damping with temperature parameter
Implement combined T1/T2 decoherence model
Create correlated two-qubit noise models
Model crosstalk between neighboring qubits
Simulate noise evolution over time
hints: Generalized amplitude damping includes thermal excitation
T2 includes both T1 and pure dephasing contributions
Correlated noise: ZZ interactions between qubits

exercises.title: Channel Fidelity Analyzer
exercises.description: Implement various channel quality metrics and compare them.
tasks: Calculate average gate fidelity
Calculate process (entanglement) fidelity
Estimate diamond distance between channels
Compare metrics for different noise types
Determine error rates from fidelity measurements
hints: F_avg = (d·F_ent + 1)/(d + 1) relates average and process fidelity
Diamond distance requires optimization (use SDP or bounds)
Compare how different noise types affect each metric
